title: Class Roster — Arrays
description: Replace single-student processing with arrays. Declare a string array for student names and a 2D double array for grades (each student has 3 grades). Use a constant MAX_STUDENTS for the array size. Loop through the arrays to enter data, calculate averages, and display all students.
test_code:
assert 'string' in cpp and ('[' in cpp.split('main')[1] or 'names' in cpp), 'Must declare a string array for student names'
cpp_after_includes = cpp.split('main')[1] if 'main' in cpp else cpp
assert 'double' in cpp and '[' in cpp, 'Must declare a double array for grades'
assert 'for' in cpp_after_includes or 'while' in cpp_after_includes, 'Must use loops to process the arrays'
assert 'MAX_STUDENTS' in cpp or 'max_students' in cpp.lower() or 'MAX' in cpp, 'Should define a constant for maximum array size'
assert cpp.count('[') >= 4, 'Must use array indexing (brackets) to access elements'
acceptance_criteria: Declares a string array for student names
Declares a double array (or 2D array) for grades
Uses a constant for the maximum number of students
Loops through arrays to enter and display data
Calls calculateAverage and getLetterGrade for each student
hints: Declare arrays: string names[MAX_STUDENTS]; double grades[MAX_STUDENTS][NUM_GRADES];
Use a for loop to fill: for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) { ... }
Pass a row of grades: calculateAverage(grades[i], NUM_GRADES)
Track how many students with an int count variable
